name: Builder Best Practices
summary: Guidelines for building effective Alberta government digital service prototypes

governmentServicePrinciples.description: Core principles for Alberta government digital services

principles.name: Plain language
principles.guideline: Write at grade 8-9 reading level. Avoid jargon and bureaucratic terms.
principles.example: Instead of 'Submit your application for adjudication', write 'Send your application. We'll review it.'

principles.name: Mobile-first
principles.guideline: All layouts must work on mobile devices. Test at 375px viewport.
principles.example: Use GoabGrid with minChildWidth instead of fixed column counts.

principles.name: Accessibility
principles.guideline: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance. Use semantic HTML, ARIA labels, proper contrast.
principles.example: Use GOA components which have accessibility built in.

principles.name: Privacy by design
principles.guideline: Include FOIP notices when collecting personal information.
principles.example: Add GoabCallout with privacy notice before any form collecting personal data.

principles.name: Clear calls to action
principles.guideline: Every page should have one primary action that's obvious.
principles.example: Landing page has single 'Start application' button in hero.

principles.name: Error prevention
principles.guideline: Validate early, show inline errors, preserve user input.
principles.example: Use form validation with clear error messages before submission.

principles.name: Status transparency
principles.guideline: Always show users where they are in a process.
principles.example: Use step indicators, progress bars, or clear status callouts.

steps.name: Start minimal
steps.guideline: Create the simplest version that demonstrates the core concept.
steps.example: Landing page with hero and single CTA before adding features.

steps.name: Show progress
steps.guideline: After each change, briefly describe what was added and ask for feedback.
steps.example: 'I added the eligibility checker page. Want me to adjust the questions?'

steps.name: Suggest enhancements
steps.guideline: Offer 2-3 specific improvements based on government service patterns.
steps.example: 'Would you like me to add a confirmation page or status tracking next?'

steps.name: Keep changes atomic
steps.guideline: One feature per edit cycle so users can validate incrementally.
steps.example: Add one page at a time, not entire multi-page flow at once.

contentGuidelines.description: Writing effective content for government services
rules: Use active voice: 'We will review your application' not 'Your application will be reviewed'
Be specific about timelines: '5-10 business days' not 'as soon as possible'
Use sentence case for headings: 'Apply for child care' not 'Apply For Child Care'
Front-load important information: lead with what users need to know
Use numbered lists for sequential steps, bullets for non-sequential items

goaComponentSelection.description: When to use which GOA components

proactiveQuestions.description: Questions to ask when starting a new service prototype

questions.question: Is this for citizens, businesses, or internal staff?
questions.whyAsk: Affects language complexity, auth requirements, navigation patterns

questions.question: What's the main action users should take? (Apply, Check status, Find info)
questions.whyAsk: Determines primary CTA and page hierarchy

questions.question: Does this service need to collect information from users?
questions.whyAsk: Determines if form integration is needed

questions.question: Are there specific eligibility requirements?
questions.whyAsk: May need eligibility checker before application

questions.question: Do users need to check application status after submitting?
